mnemonics.title: AI Problem Categories
mnemonics.content: REAP: Reasoning, Exploration, Adaptation, Planning - Remember AI solves problems by REAPing knowledge

mnemonics.title: Problem Definition Components
mnemonics.content: IGOP S (Initial state, Goal state, Operators, Path, State space) - I GET OOPS on the path to success

mnemonics.title: Knowledge Types
mnemonics.content: PDH: Procedural (How), Declarative (That/ Facts), Heuristic (Rule of thumb) - PDH helps remember the 3 types

mnemonics.title: Turing Test
mnemonics.content: T-I-M-E: Test for Intelligent Machine Existence - Turing's Imitation Machine Evaluation

acronyms.usage: The simulation of human intelligence in machines

acronyms.expansion: Initial state, Goal state, Operators, Path
acronyms.usage: Five components needed to define a search problem
Three main categories of knowledge representation

acronyms.expansion: Reasoning and Problem Solving, Knowledge and Belief, Planning and Decision Making, Learning and Adaptation
acronyms.usage: Four major categories of AI problems

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a maze where each intersection is a node (state) and each path is an edge (operator). The search explores intersections to find the exit (goal).
Think of a factory assembly line: Rules (blueprints), Working Memory (work-in-progress), Control System (foreman directing work).
Well-defined = a jigsaw puzzle with all pieces and picture reference. Ill-defined = writing a story with no guidelines.

analogies.analogy: Like a subway map - stations are states, train lines are operators connecting states, the destination station is the goal.
Like using GPS 'shortcuts' that usually work but may not always be the fastest route - it's an educated guess.
Like a treasure hunter with a map - they know starting point, treasure location, and possible paths to take.
Like a recipe: Ingredients (working memory), Recipe rules (production rules), Chef deciding what to cook (control system).
